question_text: A student performs three experiments and records observations. Which combination correctly identifies the change type and whether energy is absorbed or released?

options.label: A
options.text: Ice melting: physical change, endothermic (absorbs energy)

options.label: B
options.text: Combustion of wood: physical change, exothermic (releases energy)

options.label: C
options.text: Dissolving salt in water: chemical change, exothermic (releases energy)

options.label: D
options.text: Neutralisation of acid and base: physical change, endothermic (absorbs energy)

explanation: Ice melting is a physical change because the water molecules are still H₂O, and it absorbs heat energy from surroundings (endothermic). Option B is wrong because combustion is chemical, C is wrong because it's physical not chemical, and D is wrong because neutralisation releases heat (exothermic).
